nativeRange.habitat: Rocky subtidal and lower intertidal zones; common on rock from the lower littoral extending to subtidal depths, typically in cool-temperate to warm-temperate seas.
nativeRange.biotope: Rocky reef, subtidal rock, lower intertidal rock

decorPreferences: Rock attachment required — attach to live rock or rubble via superglue, zip-tie, or wedging; must never be buried in sand substrate

avoidWith: Herbivorous fish (tangs, rabbitfish, urchins) that graze macroalgae
High-flow environments that may tear delicate fronds
Sensitive SPS corals placed in immediate contact

imageCandidates.notes: Mosaic Macros product image showing characteristic comb-like alternate branching. Primary product image.

summary: A delicate cool-water red macroalgae with distinctive comb-like alternate branching. Requires temperatures generally advised at 24°C and below; produces halogenated terpenes that may stress nearby corals.
careNotes:
Plocamium cartilagineum is a cool-temperate to warm-temperate red alga requiring lower temperatures than most reef display species — Mosaic Macros specifies 75°F (approximately 24°C) as the upper limit, and the species has been reported to decline in warmer reef tanks. This makes it better suited to temperate or cool-reef setups than typical tropical SPS/LPS displays. It is generally advised to maintain temperatures between 18–24°C for best results.

Attachment: As a red alga (Rhodophyta), this species will melt at the base if buried in sand substrate. It must be attached to rock via superglue gel, wedging between rocks, or zip-tie/fishing line. Sand-bed placement is not suitable.

Allelopathy: Plocamium cartilagineum is one of the more extensively studied marine algae for halogenated monoterpene production. These compounds — polychlorinated and polybrominated terpenes — have been documented to have allelopathic activity and may stress sensitive corals or invertebrates placed in direct contact or close proximity. Aquarists have observed that keeping this species near SPS corals or particularly sensitive soft corals can cause irritation; placement with adequate spacing from coral colonies is generally advisable. The exact degree of impact in aquarium conditions depends on water volume, flow, and coral species.

Lighting: Full-spectrum LED lighting is recommended; actinic-only lighting cannot adequately support this species. Mosaic Macros classifies lighting needs as medium for ornamental red macros.

Flow: Gentle flow approximating soft-coral tank conditions is preferred; excessive flow can tear the delicate branched fronds.

Nutrients: Generally advised to maintain nitrate at 5–20 ppm and phosphate at 0.02–0.1 ppm. Weekly dosing of iron, iodine, and other trace micronutrients is recommended by Mosaic Macros to support healthy growth.

Calcium and Magnesium: Generally maintained at reef-standard levels — calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1,250–1,350 ppm.

Propagation: Primarily propagated by fragmentation; frond cuttings can be re-attached to rock via superglue, wedging, or zip-tie/fishing line. This is a cool-water species and Mosaic notes it requires UPS 2-Day Express shipping for live arrival guarantee, reflecting its sensitivity to thermal stress during transport.

Native ecology: Naturally found in the lower intertidal and subtidal zones (0–30 m depth) on rocky substrate across the North Atlantic, Mediterranean, and Eastern Atlantic coast from Norway/Faeroes south to Senegal and the Canary Islands.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Product page for Sea Comb (Plocamium cartilagineum). URL slug reflects an older naming convention; page content confirms Sea Comb / Plocamium cartilagineum identity. Key data: temperature ≤75°F, sold as 2-3" cuttings, red alga requiring rock attachment.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide care guide providing canonical parameter targets: temp 22–26°C (general), pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1,250–1,350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm. Confirms red algae must not be placed in sand; documents rock attachment methods. Meets 2-source minimum for Mosaic-only entries per playbook.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Plocamium cartilagineum
additional.notes: Taxonomy confirmation: Family Plocamiaceae, Order Plocamiales. Native range: British Isles, Faeroes and Norway to Senegal, Mediterranean and Canary Islands. Depth: lower littoral to 30 m. Adult size: approximately 10 cm erect thallus.
